description: A high-resolution satellite or aerial map displaying an urban landscape with an overlaid pixelated grid indicating varying land cover characteristics. The underlying imagery shows buildings, roads, parks, and what appears to be a railway yard. Overlaid on this is a grid of square pixels, approximately 10-20 metres in resolution, colour-coded using a gradient. Red pixels denote areas with the highest values of the represented characteristic, transitioning through orange and yellow for intermediate values, to light green and occasional blue pixels for the lowest values. Without a legend, the specific characteristic represented by the colour gradient is not explicitly stated, but the spatial distribution suggests it could be Imperviousness Density (IMD), Tree Cover Density (TCD), or a similar urban land cover parameter. Densely built-up blocks in the city center are predominantly coloured red and orange, while green spaces and areas adjacent to what appears to be a body of water (bottom left) are light green and blue. No scale bar, compass, legend, or specific geographic location labels are visible.
